title: Europe Faces Another Week of Dangerous Heat
subtitle: As record-breaking temperatures continue, experts and citizens question how the continent can better prepare for a hotter future.

summary.en: Europe is currently experiencing a severe and long-lasting heatwave that has pushed temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius in many regions. This extreme weather has caused significant health risks and raised questions about government preparedness. Experts note that the public is now more aware of the connection between these events and climate change. While some wonder if air conditioning is the only solution, others are calling for more long-term planning. The situation remains a major challenge for infrastructure and public safety across the continent.
summary.zh: 歐洲目前正經歷一場嚴重且持久的熱浪，許多地區氣溫已飆升至攝氏四十度以上。這種極端天氣造成了重大的健康風險，並引發了對政府準備工作的質疑。專家指出，大眾現在更清楚這些事件與氣候變遷之間的關聯。雖然有些人懷疑冷氣是否為唯一解方，但其他人則呼籲進行更長遠的規劃。對整個歐洲大陸的基礎設施與公共安全而言，這場危機依然是一項重大挑戰。

paragraphs.en: Europe is currently struggling through another week of intense, record-breaking heat. As the summer progresses, many countries are seeing temperatures rise above 40 degrees Celsius, creating a dangerous situation for public health and local infrastructure. This latest period of extreme weather follows a series of heatwaves that have already affected nations including Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic. With the heat showing few signs of stopping, officials and citizens are asking how the continent can better manage these increasingly frequent events.
paragraphs.zh: 歐洲目前正苦於又一週的酷熱天氣，氣溫屢創新高。隨著夏季推進，許多國家的氣溫飆升至攝氏四十度以上，對公共衛生與地方基礎設施造成危險。這波極端天氣緊接在德國、波蘭與捷克等國先前遭遇的一連串熱浪之後。由於高溫幾乎沒有減緩的跡象，官員與民眾都在探討歐洲大陸該如何更好地應對這些日益頻繁的氣候事件。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, this year feels different compared to previous summers. While heatwaves have become a recurring feature in Europe, the current event stands out due to its massive geographical scale and high intensity. Ajit Niranjan, an environment correspondent, noted that people are now better at connecting these extreme weather events to the science of climate change. Unlike other disasters like floods or hurricanes, the link between rising global temperatures and these heatwaves is becoming very clear to the public. The frequency of these events makes it difficult for people to ignore the situation as a one-time accident.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，今年與往年夏季感受不同。雖然熱浪已成為歐洲反覆出現的現象，但此次事件因其地理範圍廣大且強度極高而顯得格外突出。環境記者尼蘭詹（Ajit Niranjan）指出，人們現在更擅長將這些極端天氣事件與氣候變遷的科學連結起來。與洪水或颶風等其他災害不同，全球氣溫升高與這些熱浪之間的關聯，正變得對大眾而言非常清晰。這些事件的頻率使得人們難以再將其視為單一的意外事件。

paragraphs.en: Health officials have expressed deep concern regarding the impact on the population. Previous reports indicated that over 1,300 excess deaths—deaths that would not have happened under normal conditions—were linked to the heat in countries like Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic. As the heat continues, there is growing pressure on governments to explain why they were not better prepared for such extreme conditions. Many citizens are asking why, after decades of warnings from scientists, so many countries still seem surprised when the temperature rises.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生官員對此波高溫對人口造成的影響深表憂慮。先前的報告顯示，在德國、波蘭與捷克等國，有超過一千三百起超額死亡（即在正常情況下本不會發生的死亡）與高溫有關。隨著酷熱持續，政府面臨越來越大的壓力，必須解釋為何未能對此類極端狀況做好更充分的準備。許多公民質疑，在科學家提出數十年的警告後，為何當氣溫升高時，許多國家似乎仍感到措手不及。

paragraphs.en: One of the most common questions from the public is whether air conditioning is the best solution. While it provides immediate relief, experts warn that it is not a perfect answer. Relying too heavily on cooling systems can use a large amount of energy, which may make the long-term climate problem worse. Instead, there is a growing conversation about how cities can be redesigned to stay cool naturally. Some suggest that European cities need to learn from regions that have historically dealt with higher temperatures, focusing on better building designs and more green spaces to lower the heat in urban areas.
paragraphs.zh: 大眾最常見的疑問之一是，冷氣是否為最佳解決方案。雖然冷氣能提供即時緩解，但專家警告這並非完美答案。過度依賴冷卻系統會消耗大量能源，這可能會加劇長期的氣候問題。相反地，目前關於如何重新設計城市以達到自然降溫的討論正日益增加。有些人建議，歐洲城市需要向歷史上長期應對高溫的地區學習，著重於改善建築設計並增加綠地，以降低都市區域的熱度。

paragraphs.en: There is also a debate about the role of the media and politicians. Some observers feel that the media has not done enough to hold leaders accountable for their lack of preparation. As the heatwave continues, questions about the future of European politics have also emerged. Some wonder if the stress of climate change and the resulting migration issues might push political views further to the right. These complex social and political questions are now being discussed alongside the immediate need to keep people safe during the hottest days.
paragraphs.zh: 關於媒體與政治人物的角色也存在爭論。一些觀察家認為，媒體在要求領導人為準備不足負責方面做得不夠。隨著熱浪持續，關於歐洲政治未來的疑問也隨之浮現。有些人擔心，氣候變遷帶來的壓力以及隨之而來的移民問題，可能會將政治觀點進一步推向右翼。這些複雜的社會與政治議題，現在正與在酷熱日子裡確保民眾安全的迫切需求一同被討論。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the situation remains uncertain. While some people hope for a break in the weather, experts suggest that Europe must prepare for a future where these heatwaves happen more often. The current crisis has forced a conversation about whether current government responses are enough or if a more radical change in policy is required. As the continent waits for the temperatures to drop, the focus remains on protecting the most vulnerable members of society and finding ways to adapt to a changing climate.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，局勢依然充滿不確定性。雖然有些人期盼天氣能轉涼，但專家建議歐洲必須為未來熱浪更頻繁發生的情況做好準備。目前的危機迫使各界討論現行的政府應對措施是否足夠，或者是否需要更激進的政策變革。在歐洲大陸等待氣溫下降的同時，焦點仍放在保護社會中最脆弱的群體，並尋找適應氣候變遷的方法。

paragraphs.en: Ultimately, the question of whether there is a way out of this mess remains at the center of public discussion. Many are calling for a shift in how Europe handles extreme heat, moving away from reactive measures toward long-term, sustainable planning. Whether it is through improving public infrastructure, changing how buildings are constructed, or addressing the root causes of climate change, the message from experts is clear: the current way of dealing with heat is no longer enough. As the summer continues, the pressure on leaders to provide real solutions will likely grow.
paragraphs.zh: 歸根究底，是否有辦法擺脫此一困境，仍是公眾討論的核心。許多人呼籲改變歐洲處理極端高溫的方式，從被動的應對措施轉向長期的永續規劃。無論是透過改善公共基礎設施、改變建築施工方式，還是解決氣候變遷的根本原因，專家的訊息都很明確：目前應對高溫的方式已不再足夠。隨著夏季持續，要求領導人提出實質解決方案的壓力可能會越來越大。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To be much better or more noticeable than other similar things.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於描述外觀，這裡指事件的嚴重性或規模特別顯著。文中：the current event stands out due to its massive geographical scale and high intensity.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To make someone responsible for their actions or decisions.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「拿著」，這裡與 accountable 連用，表示究責。文中：Some observers feel that the media has not done enough to hold leaders accountable for their lack of preparation.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A short period of rest or a change in a situation, especially when something difficult stops temporarily.
keyTerms.definitionZh: （天氣或局勢的）暫停、緩解、轉機。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「打破」或「休息」，這裡指天氣狀況的暫時緩解。文中：While some people hope for a break in the weather, experts suggest that Europe must prepare for a future where these heatwaves happen more often.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A solution to a difficult situation or problem.
keyTerms.noteZh: 字面上是「出口」，這裡引申為解決危機的途徑。文中：Ultimately, the question of whether there is a way out of this mess remains at the center of public discussion.

sources.name: The Guardian Environment
sources.title: ‘Is there a way out of this mess?’: Your questions answered on Europe’s week of hellish heat

previousArticles.title: Europe Struggles as Record-Breaking Heatwave Continues
previousArticles.summaryEn: A dangerous heatwave is continuing to affect large parts of Europe with record-breaking temperatures. Many countries have seen temperatures rise above 40 degrees Celsius, causing significant problems for public health and infrastructure. Experts warn that these extreme events are becoming more frequent due to climate change. Governments are now facing criticism over their lack of preparation and slow response to the crisis. Officials are debating how to better protect vulnerable citizens and improve urban cooling systems for the future.

previousArticles.title: Europe Struggles as Record-Breaking Heatwave Moves East
previousArticles.summaryEn: Europe is experiencing a dangerous heatwave that has set new temperature records in several countries. Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic have all recorded temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius. Health officials report over 1,300 excess deaths linked to the extreme heat. Authorities are urging residents to stay hydrated and avoid strenuous activity. Climate change is being cited as a primary driver for these increasingly frequent and severe weather events.

previousArticles.title: Europe Faces Record-Breaking Heatwave as Temperatures Soar
previousArticles.summaryEn: Europe is currently experiencing an intense and dangerous heatwave that has set new temperature records in several countries. Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic have all reported temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius. Health officials are concerned about the rising number of deaths, particularly among older people. Authorities have issued warnings and taken emergency measures to help residents stay cool. Experts link this extreme weather to the ongoing effects of climate change.

quiz.question: What specific consequence of the heatwaves is mentioned regarding Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic?

options.A: A significant increase in migration to these countries.
options.B: A total of over 1,300 deaths that would not have occurred otherwise.
options.C: The complete failure of local power grids due to air conditioning usage.
options.D: The immediate implementation of new, radical climate change policies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出，在德國、波蘭和捷克，有超過 1,300 例「超額死亡」（excess deaths），即在正常氣候條件下本不會發生的死亡。選項 A 雖然在第五段提到氣候變遷可能影響移民問題，但並未指明這三個國家因此發生移民潮。選項 C 雖提到空調耗能，但文中並未說電網崩潰。選項 D 提到的是政府面臨壓力，而非已經實施了激進政策。

quiz.question: Based on the article, why are European citizens becoming increasingly critical of their governments?

options.A: Because governments are spending too much money on green spaces instead of infrastructure.
options.B: Because the media has failed to report on the heatwaves accurately.
options.C: Because the recurring nature of the heatwaves makes the lack of long-term preparation look like a failure.
options.D: Because citizens believe the government is responsible for creating the heatwaves.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第三段與第六段，科學家數十年來不斷發出警告，但政府似乎仍對極端高溫感到「驚訝」且缺乏準備。這讓民眾意識到，面對頻繁發生的熱浪，政府目前的反應（reactive measures）已不足夠，因此對政府長期的規劃能力產生質疑。選項 A 文中未提及。選項 B 提到媒體，但重點是媒體未監督政府，而非報導不準確。選項 D 錯誤，民眾是質疑政府的「應對準備」，而非認為政府是氣候變遷的直接製造者。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the author mean by the phrase 'way out of this mess'?

options.A: A physical exit from a crowded urban area.
options.B: A solution to the complex and difficult situation of extreme heat.
options.C: A method to physically clean up the environmental pollution.
options.D: A way to escape the political pressure from the media.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。這裡的 'mess' 是指文中描述的極端熱浪、公共衛生危機以及政府應對不力等混亂且棘手的局面。'way out' 在此語境下是指尋求解決方案。選項 A 將 'way out' 誤解為物理上的出口。選項 C 將 'mess' 誤解為字面上的髒亂，忽略了這是指氣候危機的複雜性。選項 D 雖然文中提到政治壓力，但 'way out of this mess' 指的是整體氣候與政策困境，而非單指政治壓力。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding Europe's current heat crisis?

options.A: Air conditioning is the most effective way to protect citizens from future heatwaves.
options.B: Climate change is a hoax that European politicians are using to gain more power.
options.C: Europe must shift from reactive, short-term responses to sustainable, long-term climate planning.
options.D: The media is the primary cause of the public's anxiety regarding the changing weather.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章從頭至尾都在強調，熱浪已成為常態，過去的應對方式已不足夠。第七段明確總結，歐洲需要從「反應式措施」轉向「長期、永續的規劃」，包括改善基礎設施與建築設計。選項 A 與文章第四段觀點相反，專家認為空調不是完美答案。選項 B 與文章內容完全不符。選項 D 雖提到媒體，但僅是文章的一個小支點，並非全文核心主旨。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
